info.title: Euler Data API (V3)
info.description:
API specification for Euler Data v3. This spec defines the current
resource-oriented contract for the platform, with emphasis on consistent
schemas, predictable REST semantics, caching, observability, and a uniform
developer experience.

Migration notes
- Euler Data v3 is not a path-for-path clone of v1/v2. Some endpoints map
  directly, some require multiple v3 calls, and some legacy aggregates were
  intentionally removed.
- The live [migration guide](/v3/docs/migration) explains how legacy v1/v2
  endpoints and payloads map into the v3 resource model.
- Important semantic changes:
  - Vault base APY and rewards APY are exposed separately in v3.
  - Account positions are flattened into account-vault rows instead of the
    legacy nested sub-account shape.
  - All supported API paths live under `/v3`; legacy paths return `410`.
  - Summary endpoints prefer smaller, focused resources over large
    BFF-style payloads.

Base path: /v3

Conventions
- JSON field naming: camelCase
- Addresses: accept lowercase or checksum; responses are EIP-55 checksummed
- Public APY values (`*Apy`, `apy*`): percent numbers with 6 decimal places (e.g., 5.123456 = 5.123456%)
- Contract interest-rate fields (`borrowAPY`, `supplyAPY` in `interestRates`): decimal strings in contract fraction units
- Ratios (utilization, LTV, etc.): numbers with 6 decimal places
- Response timestamps: ISO-8601 strings (UTC). Some legacy fields still expose unix
  seconds for compatibility and include `*Iso` companions.
- Query time ranges: unix seconds (from/to)

Unit policy
- v3 separates market metadata from contract verdicts by both name and type.
- Market-normalized USD prices and USD values are JSON numbers. These are values
  derived from off-chain pricing, cached price snapshots, or API-level market math.
  This includes `/v3/prices`, token price routes, vault/earn/protocol USD totals,
  portfolio market values, portfolio USD liquidation/risk fields such as
  `liabilityValueUsd`, `totalCollateralValueUsd`, `borrowLiquidationPriceUsd`,
  and `collateralLiquidationPricesUsd`, and vault holder `assetsUsd`.
- Contract-derived oracle and raw account-liquidity verdict values are exact strings.
  These preserve the integer value used by contracts, normalized to 18 decimals
  for API exposure. Oracle prices are quoted in the vault unit of account, which
  can be USD, ETH, or another asset. This includes `/v3/oracles/prices`, raw
  account-liquidity `liabilityValue` and `totalCollateralValue` objects,
  `liabilityValueBorrowing`, `liabilityValueLiquidation`, and collateral verdict
  values.
- On-chain token quantities are bigint strings in their native unit scale. This
  includes amounts, balances, shares, assets, borrows, reward amounts, liquidation
  repay/yield amounts, and block numbers.
- Caps are also bigint strings, but EVK caps are exposed as resolved underlying
  asset-unit amounts rather than packed contract config words.
- LTV config values use canonical basis-point strings. Legacy decimal aliases
  may remain on config-history payloads for compatibility when explicitly named.

Rate limiting
- Optional API keys for higher limits
- Default limits are configurable; current defaults: free tier 100 req/min (IP-based)
  and authenticated 1000 req/min (per API key), 60s window.
- By default, proxy headers are not trusted for IP identity unless explicitly enabled.
- Redis failures default to fail-closed (503) for strict enforcement; fail-open is opt-in.
- RateLimit-* (IETF draft-6) and legacy X-RateLimit-* headers are returned.

CORS
- Public API responses use wildcard CORS, including preflight responses for unsafe methods.
- Unsafe methods with request bodies must use a JSON media type.

Caching
- Cache-Control headers are returned for cacheable endpoints.
- Defaults (may be adjusted in config):
  - Prices: 300s
  - APYs: 600s
  - Rewards: 900s
  - General responses: 60s (short 30s, long 300s)

Errors
- All errors use a standard envelope: { error: { code, message, requestId, details? } }
- Domain error codes are documented in this spec.
- JSON request bodies must use `Content-Type: application/json` or another `application/*+json` media type.
- Request bodies larger than 1 MiB are rejected before route parsing with `413 PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE`.

tags.name: Protocol
tags.description: Protocol-wide summary endpoints and aggregate metrics. In v3, these endpoints are explicitly summary-oriented and should be treated as lightweight rollups rather than replacements for full vault list payloads.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Liveness, readiness, and dashboard-friendly platform status checks. Use these for operational monitoring, not for reconstructing full portfolio state.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication and authorization conventions used across the API, including API keys, admin secrets, and rate-limit behavior.

tags.name: Entities
tags.description: Platform-admin managed KYBed organization profiles used as backend-owned label and control-plane resources.

tags.name: Curator
tags.description: Curator-managed metadata such as labels, descriptions, and visibility controls. This is the operator-owned customization surface in v3.

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: API usage and API key analytics. These endpoints are intended for operators and consumers tracking traffic, rate limits, and key-level activity.

tags.name: Chains
tags.description: Chain inventory and per-chain protocol summaries. These replace ad-hoc chain-level aggregates from legacy APIs with explicit, cacheable resources.

tags.name: Vaults
tags.description: Canonical vault resources, vault history, holders, borrowers, and open-interest views. In v3, vault detail is decomposed into focused endpoints instead of one large legacy response.

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: Account activity, discovered positions, and sub-account relationships. Legacy nested account payloads are flattened into account-vault rows in v3.

tags.name: Tokens
tags.description: Token metadata, protocol classifications, and related static reference data used throughout the API.

tags.name: Prices
tags.description: Current and historical token prices. Prices follow v3 source priority rules and return the canonical merged value rather than exposing raw provider-specific reads by default.

tags.name: APYs
tags.description: Base and intrinsic APY surfaces. In v3, APY families are split into dedicated endpoints instead of being embedded inconsistently across larger payloads.

tags.name: Rewards
tags.description: Reward campaign APRs and per-account reward breakdowns. Rewards APY is intentionally separate from base vault APY in v3; combine them client-side when you need a legacy-style total supply APY.

tags.name: Oracles
tags.description: Oracle routing, adapter, and price-source inspection endpoints. These expose pricing/oracle internals that were previously harder to inspect externally.

tags.name: Earn
tags.description: Euler Earn vault resources and historical data. These endpoints are separate from EVK vaults and should be modeled independently in client code.

tags.name: Liquidations
tags.description: Liquidation events and related historical views used for risk analysis and monitoring.

tags.name: TermsOfUse
tags.description: Terms-of-use signature state and audit endpoints.

tags.name: EVC
tags.description: Ethereum Vault Connector account event resources. These are separate from account position snapshots and are intended for event-level inspection.

tags.name: PublicAllocator
tags.description: Public allocator event resources for allocator-level activity and history.

tags.name: FeeFlow
tags.description: Fee-flow event resources and fee-related historical activity.

tags.name: GraphQL
tags.description: Controlled GraphQL passthrough endpoints for indexer-backed data exploration and compatibility workflows.

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key authentication (optional; higher rate limits).

BearerAuth.description: Alternative to X-API-Key using Authorization: Bearer <key>

AdminSecret.name: X-Admin-Secret
AdminSecret.description: Admin secret (server-to-server) for API key management.

PlatformSession.name: euler_platform_session
PlatformSession.description: Signed, httpOnly platform-operator browser session cookie.

PlatformCsrf.name: X-CSRF-Token
PlatformCsrf.description: Double-submit CSRF token required for unsafe cookie-authenticated methods.

ChainIdParam.name: chainId
ChainIdParam.description: Comma-separated list of chain IDs (single value allowed).

LimitParam.name: limit
LimitParam.description: Requested page size. Values above the endpoint cap are clamped; the default shared cap is 100.

TokenLimitParam.name: limit
TokenLimitParam.description: Requested token page size. Values above 500 are clamped.

FieldsParam.name: fields
FieldsParam.description: Comma-separated list of fields to include.

IncludeParam.name: include
IncludeParam.description: Comma-separated list of related resources to expand. Unknown values are ignored.

SortParam.name: sort
SortParam.description: Sort field, prefix with '-' for descending.

BlockNumberParam.name: blockNumber
BlockNumberParam.description: Block number (string to avoid precision limits).

code.description:
Domain error code. Examples:
- INVALID_ADDRESS
- CHAIN_NOT_SUPPORTED
- VAULT_NOT_FOUND
- TOKEN_NOT_FOUND
- ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND
- VALIDATION_ERROR
- RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE
- UNAUTHORIZED
- FORBIDDEN
- INTERNAL_ERROR

googleEmail.description: Google Workspace email address to grant or revoke.

address.description: EIP-55 checksummed governance address in API responses.
Valid EVM address. Lowercase or correctly checksummed mixed-case input is accepted.

total.description: Exact total count when the endpoint provides one.

hasMore.description: Indicates whether another page exists beyond the current page.

limit.description: Echoed page size after endpoint-side clamping.

chainId.description: Comma-separated chain IDs for multi-chain responses.

ProtocolStats.description: EVK lending aggregates across the selected chains, with Earn TVL reported separately.

totalSuppliedUsd.description: Total EVK lending supply in USD. This is the sum of vault `cash + totalBorrows` valued in USD and excludes Earn vault assets.

totalBorrowedUsd.description: Total EVK lending borrows in USD across the selected chains.

earnTotalAssetsUsd.description: Total Earn vault assets in USD. Reported separately and not included in `totalSuppliedUsd` or `utilization`.

utilization.description: EVK lending utilization, computed as `totalBorrowedUsd / totalSuppliedUsd`. Earn assets are excluded from the denominator.

chainId.description: Chain ID for the returned chain-scoped aggregate.

OraclePriceRaw.description: Exact oracle quote values returned by contracts, represented as raw integer strings in the oracle unit-of-account scale.

amountIn.description: Raw input amount passed to the oracle.

accumulatedFeesShares.description: Raw accumulated fee shares as a bigint string.

accumulatedFeesAssets.description: Raw accumulated fee assets as a bigint string.

supplyCap.description: Resolved supply cap as a bigint string in underlying asset units.

borrowCap.description: Resolved borrow cap as a bigint string in underlying asset units.

VaultInterestRates.description: Contract interest-rate values formatted as decimal strings in fraction units, not public percent APY numbers.

borrowSPY.description: Borrow rate per second as an exact decimal string.

borrowAPY.description: Borrow APY as an exact decimal fraction string; `1` means 100%.

supplyAPY.description: Supply APY as an exact decimal fraction string; `1` means 100%.

vaultType.description: Vault family for the `/v3/evk/vaults` EVK surface.

totalAssets.description: Raw underlying asset amount as a bigint string.

totalBorrows.description: Raw borrowed asset amount as a bigint string.

totalSupplyUsd.description: Market USD number for total supplied assets.

totalBorrowsUsd.description: Market USD number for total borrowed assets.

utilization.description: Utilization ratio number, computed from market-normalized totals.

supplyApy.description: Public supply APY as a percent number.

borrowApy.description: Public borrow APY as a percent number.

governor.description: Latest governor admin if available; falls back to creator.

governorAdmin.description: Latest governor admin if available; falls back to creator.

collateralValueLiquidation.description: Contract-derived collateral liquidation value, raw 18-decimal bigint string.

borrowing.description: Borrowing APY contribution as a percent number.

rewards.description: Rewards APY contribution as a percent number.

intrinsicApy.description: Intrinsic APY contribution as a percent number.

borrowed.description: Raw borrowed asset amount as a bigint string.

marketPriceUsd.description: Market USD price number for this position's asset.

suppliedValueUsd.description: Market USD value number for this supplied position.

borrowedValueUsd.description: Market USD value number for this borrowed position.

collateralLiquidationPricesUsd.description: Liquidation prices in USD keyed by collateral address.

supplyApy.description: Public supply APY as a percent number.

borrowApy.description: Public borrow APY as a percent number.

supplyApy1h.description: One-hour public supply APY as a percent number.

assets.description: Raw underlying asset amount as a bigint string.

shares.description: Raw vault share amount as a bigint string.

logoURI.description: Preferred token image URI. Served from the Euler token image CDN with an hourly cache-busting query param.

priceUsd.description: Market USD price number when token list responses include a price.

tags.description: CoinGecko category and symbol-derived correlation tags used by clients for asset-specific processing.

TokenMetadata.description: Protocol integration metadata for a token.

priceUsd.description: Market-normalized USD price number. If `source` is `oracle`, this endpoint still exposes the v3 market-price number; use `/v3/oracles/prices` for exact contract-derived oracle values.

apr.description: Reward APR contribution as a public percent number.

status.description: Campaign status. 'active' = ongoing, 'upcoming' = not yet started, 'ended' = past.

merklCampaignId.description: Original Merkl campaign id when the public campaign is fanned out into vault-level instances.

opportunityType.description: Merkl opportunity type that produced this campaign.

distributionType.description: Merkl distribution type for the campaign.

whitelist.description: Lowercase recipient addresses eligible for whitelist-gated campaigns.

blacklist.description: Lowercase recipient addresses excluded from blacklist-gated campaigns.

totalApr.description: Sum of currently active reward APR contributions as a public percent number.

rewardsApy.description: Rewards APY as a public percent number.

timestamp.description: Bucket timestamp as an ISO-8601 UTC string.

amount.description: Raw reward token amount as a bigint string.

adapter.description: Preferred public field name for the whitelisted oracle adapter address.

element.description: Legacy internal field name for the adapter address.

apy.description: Intrinsic APY as a percent number.

apyDuration.description: Duration in seconds used to compute the APY.

tvlUsd.description: Market USD total value locked number.

CollateralConfig.description: Current LTV configuration for a collateral vault

vaultType.description: Vault family for the collateral address

asset.description: Underlying asset address of the collateral vault

borrowLTV.description: Maximum LTV for borrowing (basis points as string, e.g., "8500" = 85%)

liquidationLTV.description: LTV at which liquidation can occur (basis points as string)

targetTimestamp.description: ISO timestamp when LTV ramp completes

rampDuration.description: Duration of LTV ramp in seconds

targetTimestamp.description: ISO timestamp when LTV ramp completes

rampDuration.description: Duration of LTV ramp in seconds

logIndex.description: Log index within the transaction receipt.

borrowCap.description: Borrow cap in resolved asset units

thresholdExceeded.description: Whether `capUsage` meets or exceeds the requested threshold

data.description: The configuration values that changed (varies by type). For `ltv` events, canonical basis-point string fields such as `borrowLTV` may be accompanied by legacy decimal aliases.

logIndex.description: Log index within the transaction receipt.

get.description: Redirects the browser to Google OAuth/OIDC and sets a signed OAuth state cookie.

302.description: Redirect to configured Google OAuth authorization URL.

Set-Cookie.description: Signed OAuth state cookie with httpOnly, SameSite, path, and expiry.

get.description: Validates state, nonce, Google ID-token claims, and active platform-admin access.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: OAuth authorization code. Never returned in responses.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: OAuth anti-forgery state bound to the signed state cookie.

302.description: Successful callback; session and CSRF cookies are set, then browser is redirected.

Set-Cookie.description: Signed httpOnly session cookie, readable CSRF cookie, and cleared OAuth state cookie.

400.description: Invalid callback, state, nonce, or ID token.

403.description: Google identity is not an active platform admin.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired platform-admin session.

403.description: Authenticated session exists, but platform-admin access is not active.
Missing or mismatched CSRF token, or inactive platform-admin access.

post.description:
Creates an active platform-admin access row for a Google email address.
Requires a valid platform session and CSRF token because this is an unsafe
cookie-authenticated write.

200.description: Existing revoked platform-admin access was reactivated.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired platform-admin session.

403.description: Inactive platform-admin access, missing CSRF token, or mismatched CSRF token.

post.description:
Soft-revokes platform-admin access for a Google email address. Requires a
valid platform session and CSRF token because this is an unsafe
cookie-authenticated write.

riskTier.description: Plain text only; clients must render as text, not HTML or Markdown.

strategyDescription.description: Plain text only; clients must render as text, not HTML or Markdown. Tabs and line breaks are allowed.

get.summary: Protocol-wide EVK lending stats across chains
get.description: Returns EVK lending aggregates across the selected chains. `earnTotalAssetsUsd` is reported separately and is not part of `totalSuppliedUsd` or `utilization`.
Returns chain-scoped EVK lending aggregates. `earnTotalAssetsUsd` is reported separately and is not part of `totalSuppliedUsd` or `utilization`.
Legacy location for this collection.

Returns a list of vaults.
Notes:
  - `fields` and `sort` are accepted but currently ignored.
  - `minTvl`/`maxTvl` apply to `totalSupplyUsd` where `totalSupplyUsd = (cash + totalBorrows) * price`.

parameters.name: visibility
parameters.description: Comma-separated visibility filter (`visible,warning,hidden,pending_review`).

post.description:
Returns canonical EVK vault detail entities for an explicit address set.
Use this to avoid high client-side fanout when you already know the vault
addresses you want to render.

Notes:
  - Request order is preserved for found vaults.
  - `meta.notFound` returns requested addresses that were not resolved.
  - `include=["collaterals"]` expands collateral detail for every returned vault.
  - The request cap is 1000 vault addresses and 256 KiB of JSON body.

get.summary: Resolve a vault address to its family
get.description: Resolve a single `(chainId, address)` pair to `vaultType`, `factory`, and the canonical resource path when one exists.

post.summary: Resolve multiple vault addresses to their families
post.description: Bulk resolver for SDK/meta-routing use cases. Supports up to 500 addresses per request.

get.description:
Lists vaults ordered by utilization severity (descending).
- `chainId` is required.
- If `threshold` is omitted, returns all vaults.
- Threshold filter is inclusive (`utilization >= threshold`).
- `onlyOverThreshold` is accepted for compatibility and currently does not alter filtering.

Lists vaults ordered by cap-usage severity (descending).
Cap usage is computed as `max(supplyCapUsage, borrowCapUsage)`, and the
response includes both supply-cap and borrow-cap fields.
- `chainId` is required.
- If `threshold` is omitted, returns all vaults.
- Threshold filter is inclusive (`capUsage >= threshold`).
- `onlyOverThreshold` is accepted for compatibility and currently does not alter filtering.

Returns vault detail. Optional `include` expands related resources in a single response.
Supported values: `collaterals`, `apy`, `totals`, `prices`.
The single-vault detail response uses `totalBorrowed` as the canonical borrow-total field.

get.summary: Public curator labels for a vault
Deterministic visibility status for a vault

200.description: Current visibility status and check results

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: Defaults to `1d`. If omitted, uses last 30 days for `1d` and last 7 days for `1h`.

parameters.name: addressPrefix
parameters.description: Optional 4-byte account prefix filter (`0x` + 8 hex chars).

parameters.name: blockNumber
parameters.description: Not supported for this endpoint. If provided, returns 400.

get.description:
Returns a position-first portfolio view for an owner on a single chain.
The backend builds SDK Account and Portfolio entities from internal V3
account, vault, price, and label data sources; it does not recursively
call `/v3/accounts/{address}/positions`. When `forceFresh=true`, the
endpoint enqueues or promotes account snapshot refresh work and waits
briefly for refreshed snapshots before falling back to the current
indexed portfolio view. Freshness metadata indicates whether the refresh
completed, timed out, or was rate-limited.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Chain ID. Exactly one chain is required.

parameters.name: labeledVaultsOnly
parameters.description: Filter positions to the product-facing labeled vault universe.

parameters.name: includeAccount
parameters.description: Include the backing SDK account-shaped payload for debugging and migration.

parameters.name: forceFresh
parameters.description:
When true, enqueue or promote account snapshot refresh for discovered tuples.
The endpoint waits briefly for refreshed snapshots before falling back to
the current indexed portfolio view. Inspect `data.freshness` for completion,
timeout, rate-limit, and fallback metadata.

get.description:
Returns paginated account positions across the selected chain scope.
Uses `meta.hasMore` for pagination continuation on the hot path rather than an exact `meta.total`.

parameters.name: forceFresh
parameters.description:
When true, trigger bounded synchronous account snapshot refresh for the returned tuples.
On timeout/failure, response safely falls back to stored snapshots and indicates cached mode in freshness metadata.

get.description:
Discovers vault tuples an account interacts with (shares and/or controller-enabled debt),
including sub-accounts when available.
Uses `meta.hasMore` for pagination continuation on the hot path rather than an exact `meta.total`.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Filter by a token address. May be repeated. Combine with chainId for the fastest lookup path. Counts toward the 100-address filter cap. When address filters are present and limit is omitted, the default limit is the requested address count capped at 100.

parameters.name: addresses
parameters.description: Filter by comma-separated token addresses. May be repeated. Maximum 100 addresses. Combine with chainId for the fastest lookup path. When address filters are present and limit is omitted, the default limit is the requested address count capped at 100.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by token classification. Comma-separated values: base, debt, unitOfAccount, oracleAsset, reward, swapAsset

parameters.name: addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated token addresses (max 100).

parameters.name: assets
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for `addresses`.

get.description:
Historical prices for explicit token addresses over a bounded time range.
Required query params: `chainId`, `addresses`, `from`, `to`.
Maximum allowed window is 30 days per request.

parameters.name: addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated token addresses.

parameters.name: assets
parameters.description: Deprecated alias for `addresses`.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Inclusive start of the history window as a unix timestamp in seconds (UTC).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Inclusive end of the history window as a unix timestamp in seconds (UTC).

parameters.name: asset
parameters.description: Asset address to query.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: Bucket size for aggregation.

get.description:
Rewards APY history for a required time range.
Required query params: `chainId`, `from`, `to`.
Optional filters: `vault`, `resolution`, `offset`, `limit`.
Maximum allowed window is 30 days per request.

Supports exact mode (`chainId + vault + asset`) and list mode (`chainId` + pagination).
- Exact mode requires the full key.
- Exact mode returns `200` with `price: null` when the tuple is known but currently unpriced.
- Non-null `price` values are exact contract-derived prices encoded as 18-decimal bigint strings in `unitOfAccount` terms.
- Exact mode returns `404` only when the `(chainId, vault, asset)` tuple is unknown.
- Partial exact key requests return `400`.
- List mode returns flat rows sorted by `vault ASC, asset ASC`.

Factory-deployed EulerRouter list with latest indexed config and resolved-vault state.
- `chainId` is required.
- Optional filters: `vault`, `adapter`, `offset`, `limit`.

Distinct oracle adapter addresses ever configured through `ConfigSet` events
on any EulerRouter deployed by OracleRouterFactory for the requested chain.
- `chainId` is required.
- Supports `offset` and `limit`.

post.description:
Returns earn vault summaries for explicit addresses on one chain.
Pass `include: ["strategies"]` to add the same strategy objects
returned by earn vault detail.
Response preserves request order after address deduplication.
Request cap: 1000 vault addresses and 256 KiB of JSON body.

get.description:
Returns earn vault detail. Optional `include` expands related resources.
Supported values: `apy`, `totals`, `prices`.

get.summary: Unified Euler Earn vault event timeline
get.description:
Unified stream of user, interest/reallocation, and governance earn-vault events.
Requires a bounded time window (`from` + `to`), max 30 days.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated event type filter. Unknown values return 400.

get.description:
Returns a unified timeline of all configuration changes for this vault,
including caps, LTV, IRM, governor, fee receiver, hook config, and other settings.
For specific config types, use the dedicated endpoints (cap-history, ltv-history, irm-history).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by configuration change type

get.summary: Current accepted collaterals with LTV configurations
get.description:
Returns the list of vaults accepted as collateral for this vault,
along with their current LTV configurations. Use this to understand
what assets can be used as collateral when borrowing from this vault.

parameters.name: collateral
parameters.description: Filter by collateral vault address

get.description: Returns historical changes to supply and borrow caps for this vault.
Returns historical changes to the interest rate model for this vault.

Unified stream of deposit, withdraw, borrow, repay, and transfer events for one vault.
Requires a bounded time window (`from` + `to`), max 30 days.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated event type filter. Unknown values return 400.

get.description: Returns totalAssets, totalBorrows, utilization, APY from dynamic snapshots. Historical values are raw amounts - compose with /v3/prices for USD conversion.

parameters.name: forceFresh
parameters.description:
When true, trigger bounded synchronous vault dynamic refresh before reading totals.
On timeout/failure, response safely falls back to stored snapshots and indicates cached mode in freshness metadata.

200.description: Vault totals with current + historical data

get.description: Returns all holders with non-zero share balances via transfer aggregation (raw holder set). This endpoint does not unwrap Euler Earn depositors and therefore aligns with legacy behavior when `resolveEulerEarn=false`. Supports 4-byte `addressPrefix` filter for wallet-scoped scans. Balance values are integer strings in raw mode; when resolve flags are enabled, balances may be proportional decimal strings.

parameters.name: addressPrefix
parameters.description: Optional 4-byte account prefix filter (`0x` + 8 hex chars).

parameters.name: resolveEulerEarn
parameters.description: When true, expands Euler Earn vault-holder addresses into underlying depositors (v1 parity mode).

parameters.name: resolveSubAccounts
parameters.description: When true, aggregates sub-account holders into their owner addresses.

balance.description: Numeric string; integer in raw mode, may be decimal when resolve flags are enabled.

get.description: Returns addresses that have the vault enabled as a controller. Optimistic set - some may have zero debt.

Event-based aggregation of total borrows and borrower counts. Uses MV-backed query path (`open_interest_mv`) with safe fallback to raw aggregation when unavailable. Prices should be composed separately via /v3/prices.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Chain ID (required)

get.description: Collateral-covered USD borrow exposure as a nested map keyed by borrow vault, then collateral vault. Underwater debt is capped at current attributable collateral value, so this endpoint is not gross open interest. The expensive account-level debt/collateral composition is worker-computed and stored in a latest projection table; the request path only reads that projection. Redis may cache this response, but PostgreSQL projection rows are the source of truth.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Chain ID (required)

additionalProperties.description: USD collateral-covered debt attributable to this collateral vault

data.description: Borrow-vault to collateral-vault USD collateral-covered exposure map

get.description: Borrow-vault aggregate of asset-value bad debt. Rows include only underwater account/borrow-vault positions where gross debt value exceeds the current value of enabled collateral considered for that debt position. Healthy accounts are omitted and no chain-level bad-debt total is exposed. Values use asset-value insolvency, not risk-adjusted LTV shortfall.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Chain ID (required)

parameters.name: borrowVault
parameters.description: Optional borrow vault/controller address filter

parameters.name: minBadDebtUsd
parameters.description: Minimum bad debt USD threshold. Defaults to 0.

accountCount.description: Number of underwater accounts contributing to this borrow-vault row.

debtUsd.description: Gross USD debt for underwater account/borrow-vault positions.

collateralUsd.description: Current enabled collateral value considered for those underwater positions.

coveredDebtUsd.description: Portion of debt covered by current collateral value.

badDebtUsd.description: Uncovered debt, equal to debtUsd minus coveredDebtUsd within tolerance.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of chain IDs (single value allowed).

get.description: Account-primary EVC event stream. `chainId`, `from`, and `to` are required.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Comma-separated chain IDs.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated event type filter. Unknown values return 400.

get.description: Unified stream of public allocator events. `chainId`, `from`, and `to` are required.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Comma-separated chain IDs.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated event type filter. Unknown values return 400.

get.description:
Unified stream of fee-flow events with current indexed coverage (`buy`).
`chainId`, `from`, and `to` are required.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Comma-separated chain IDs.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Comma-separated event type filter. Current supported value is `buy`. Unknown values return 400.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Optional signer/account filter.

get.summary: Check whether an address signed terms of use

get.description:
Lightweight GET mode for development/debugging.
Disabled in production; use POST `/v3/graphql` there.
Requires `query` and optionally accepts `variables` (JSON string) and `operationName`.
Ponder LIKE filter operators reject unescaped `%` and `_` wildcard values.
For standard GraphQL usage, use POST `/v3/graphql`.

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: JSON-encoded variables object.

post.description:
GraphQL endpoint (shared auth/rate limit with REST), proxied to the
Ponder GraphQL API.
Ponder LIKE filter operators reject unescaped `%` and `_` wildcard values.
